blurb:
This little spot on Roswell Road is something different. It's a Brazilian a脙搂a脙颅 shop where you build your own bowl and load it up with whatever toppings you want. The vibe is warm and cozy, and the staff actually seems happy to be there. Great for an afternoon treat or a lighter lunch.

The self-serve a脙搂a脙颅 bowls are the main draw, but they also have crepes and coxinha if you want something more savory. Everything we tried was fresh and really good. With a 4.9 rating and a small but loyal following, this place is a hidden gem worth knowing about.

They're closed on Tuesdays, so keep that in mind.

blurb:
If you live near Roswell Road and need a solid pizza spot, JJ's is the answer. It's casual and affordable, and the kind of place you keep coming back to because they never let you down. Good for a weeknight dinner or grabbing a pie for the family on a Friday.

The stuffed pizza is what people are talking about right now, and for good reason. One slice is genuinely a full meal, so don't over-order. Portions are generous and the price is right.

Just note they're closed on Tuesdays, so plan around that.

blurb:
This place has some of the best pho in the area, and the owner is genuinely one of the nicest people you will meet. He takes time to explain the food and how they make things, which makes the whole visit feel more personal than your average lunch spot. Good for a casual lunch or an easy weeknight dinner when you want something warm and filling without spending much.

The pho is made the traditional way, and you can taste the difference. If you have never tried pho before, this is a great place to start. Prices are very reasonable, and they are open every day, which is a bonus.

Parking is in the shopping center lot off Roswell Road, easy in and out.

blurb:
Canton Cooks is a good spot to keep in your back pocket when you want Chinese food that goes beyond the usual takeout options. The menu is more interesting than most, with things like lobster in black bean sauce, BBQ duck, and hot pot if you are feeling adventurous. Good for a casual family dinner or if you want to try something a little different without making a big night of it.

The BBQ duck and the lobster in black bean sauce are what set this place apart from the typical strip mall Chinese spots. It is worth ordering something off the specials side of the menu rather than sticking to the basics.

They are open until 10pm every night, which makes it a solid option when you are thinking about dinner later in the evening.

blurb:
Royal Spice is where my wife and I go when we want a real sit-down Indian dinner without driving all the way into the city. The staff is friendly and patient, which is great if you are not super familiar with Indian food and want to ask questions about the menu. Good for a date night or a low-key dinner out.

The entrees are the highlight here, and the appetizers are worth ordering before your main course. The servers are happy to walk you through what's on the menu, so do not be shy about asking.

Note that they close between lunch and dinner on weekdays, reopening at 4:30pm. Not the place if you are looking for a late lunch on a Tuesday.

blurb:
Sunnyside is one of those places I keep recommending to neighbors and they always come back thanking me for it. It's a solid neighborhood pizza spot with a relaxed vibe. Great for a Friday night with the family or grabbing a pie to take home on a weeknight.

The Hot Honey Charred pizza is what most people are talking about, and for good reason. The Hot Honey Lemon Pepper Chicken Wings are worth ordering too -- the sauce is sweet with just enough heat, and people apparently dip their crust right into whatever is left. The Caesar Salad is simple and done well if you want something to start.

Just know they are closed on Mondays, so plan accordingly.

blurb:
J. Christopher's is the kind of breakfast spot you end up going back to every few weeks without really planning it. It's casual, it's consistent, and it does classic morning food well. Good for a weekend family breakfast or a quick weekday morning before work.

They serve all the usual favorites, from omelets to burgers, plus seasonal specials that change things up a bit. Nothing too fancy, just solid comfort food done right.

They're open every day but only until 2pm, so this is strictly a breakfast and lunch place. Don't show up thinking you'll squeeze in a late afternoon meal.

blurb:
Los Bravos has been a go-to for a lot of people in this area for a while, and the food from the kitchen is still the reason to go. It's a reliable Mexican spot right on Roswell Road, open seven days a week, and good for a casual family dinner or a quick lunch out.

The food consistently gets high marks from regulars. Just be aware that the margaritas and bar drinks have gotten pricey lately. Long-time customers have noticed the jump, so if you're watching the tab, stick to the food and you'll walk away happy.
